meta.disclaimer: AI-generated proof-of-concept questions. NOT real MCAT/AAMC content. Text-only (no HTML/SVG). Each item has exactly one correct answer and three distinct distractors; all items passed the 4-criterion automated eval.

questions.stem: In genetics, what is meant by the term locus?

choices.label: A
choices.text: having two identical alleles at a locus

choices.label: B
choices.text: the observable characteristics of an organism

choices.label: C
choices.text: an allele expressed even in the heterozygous state

choices.label: D
choices.text: the physical location of a gene on a chromosome

questions.explanation: locus: the physical location of a gene on a chromosome
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: In evolution, what is a living fossil?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a species that has changed little over long spans of geologic time

choices.label: B
choices.text: a situation in which improving one trait comes at a cost to another

choices.label: C
choices.text: the use of mutation rate to estimate divergence times between lineages

choices.label: D
choices.text: a species found naturally in only one geographic region

questions.explanation: a living fossil is a species that has changed little over long spans of geologic time.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: In eukaryotic cells, what is the primary function of the peroxisome?

choices.label: B

choices.label: C

choices.label: D
choices.text: acts as the selective barrier controlling what enters and leaves the cell

questions.stem: An enzyme's inhibition can be overcome by adding much more substrate, and Vmax is unchanged while apparent Km rises. What type of inhibitor is this?
questions.explanation: Competitive inhibitors compete with substrate for the active site; excess substrate outcompetes them, so Vmax is unchanged but apparent Km increases.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: In a growth experiment, a bacterial culture shows no increase in cell number for the first hour after inoculation into fresh medium. Which growth phase is this?
